content.problemStatement:
This is the easy version of TOOFAR, and is worth 404040 points.

An array AAA of length MMM is said to be prefix-balanced under the following conditions:


Let SAS_ASA​ denote the set of all elements that appear in AAA.
For each x∈SAx \in S_Ax∈SA​, and index iii (1≤i≤M1 \leq i \leq M1≤i≤M), let fi(x)f_i(x)fi​(x) denote the number of times xxx appears among [A1,A2,…,Ai][A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_i][A1​,A2​,…,Ai​].

That is, fi(x)f_i(x)fi​(x) denotes the frequency of xxx in AAA's prefix of length iii.
Then, AAA is called prefix-balanced if, for **every** triple of integers (x,y,i)(x, y, i)(x,y,i) such that x,y∈SAx, y \in S_Ax,y∈SA​ and 1≤i≤M1 \leq i \leq M1≤i≤M, we have

∣fi(x)−fi(y)∣≤1|f_i(x) - f_i(y)| \leq 1∣fi​(x)−fi​(y)∣≤1
For example, the arrays [1,2,1][1, 2, 1][1,2,1] and [1,3,2,3][1, 3, 2, 3][1,3,2,3] are prefix-balanced, but [2,3,2,4][2, 3, 2, 4][2,3,2,4] is not (the prefix of length 333 contains two occurrences of 222 and no occurrences of 444).

You are given two integers NNN and MMM.

There are MNM^NMN arrays of length NNN whose elements lie between 111 and MMM. How many of them are prefix-balanced?

The answer might be large, so find it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of arrays of prefix-balanced arrays of length NNN, whose elements lie in [1,M][1, M][1,M]. Report the answer modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of MMM over all test cases both won't exceed 5⋅1055\cdot 10^55⋅105.
